title: UGC NET Eligibility 2026: Nationality

detail.title: UGC NET Eligibility 2026: Nationality
detail.short_info: Check detailed educational qualification, age limit for JRF & Assistant Professor, and category-wise marks requirements. Candidates must have a Master's degree with 55% marks (50% for reserved categories) to apply for UGC NET June 2026.

content.text: The reservation policy for UGC NET 2026 follows the Government of India norms to ensure equal opportunities for candidates from different categories. Conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA) on behalf of the University Grants Commission (UGC), the exam provides qualification for Junior Research Fellowship (JRF), Assistant Professor, and PhD admissions.

As per the official guidelines, reservation in central universities and deemed institutions is provided for SC, ST, OBC-NCL, EWS, and PwD categories. This policy also extends benefits in qualifying marks and age relaxation (especially for JRF). Understanding the detailed reservation criteria is essential for candidates to claim their benefits accurately during application and counselling.

rows: UGC NET (University Grants Commission – National Eligibility Test)

content.text: Candidates must be Indian nationals. They need valid proof of Indian citizenship such as Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Passport, or any other government-issued photo ID.
Candidates must possess a Master’s degree or equivalent from a UGC-recognized university/institution. The subject chosen for Paper 2 should generally match the postgraduate subject.

items: General/EWS category: Minimum 55% marks (without rounding off).
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/SC/ST/PwD/Third Gender: Minimum 50% marks.
Final-year Master’s students or those with results awaited can apply provisionally.
Candidates with a 4-year UG degree (75% marks or equivalent; 70% for reserved) may be eligible in select cases.

sections.heading: UGC NET Eligibility 2026: Age Limit for JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) and Assistant Professor

items: Junior Research Fellowship (JRF): Maximum 30 years as on the cut-off date specified in the notification.
Assistant Professor: No upper age limit. Candidates of any age can apply.

sections.heading: UGC NET Eligibility 2026: Number of Attempts

content.text: There is no limit on the number of attempts. Candidates can appear as many times as they wish, subject to the age limit for JRF.

The reservation policy of the Government of India applies to UGC NET. According to this, in the Central Universities and Institutions deemed to be universities, the reservation of seats is as follows:

items: 15% of the seats for the Scheduled Castes (SC)
7.5% of the seats for the Scheduled Tribes (ST)
27% for Other Backward Classes (OBC) Non-Creamy Layer (NCL)
10% reservation for EWS (Economically Weaker Section)
5% for PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability

sections.heading: How to Apply for UGC NET 2026 (Step-by-Step)

items: Visit the official website ugcnet.nta.nic.in
Register with valid email and mobile number
Fill the online application form
Upload scanned documents (photo, signature, etc.)
Pay the application fee
Submit and download the confirmation page

seo_tags: UGC NET Eligibility Criteria 2026: Check Age Limit, Educational Qualification and Age Limit
